description: TRENDING regime: apply_regime_exit_adjustment reduces the confirmation ticks below the base EXIT_CONFIRM_TICKS, making the exit more active (tighter confirmation, consistent with Kaminski-Lo 2014 trending -> follow through).

derivation.spec_basis: PHASE3_DECISIONS.md §Regime->response mapping: TRENDING -> more-active. More-active means fewer confirmation ticks required before the stop fires. Direction: Kaminski-Lo (2014) — returns PERSIST in trending regimes, so a stop trigger is more likely to be a genuine trend reversal than a noise spike. Restraint is counterproductive when momentum is real.
derivation.tolerance_note: Expected value is an integer. Exact equality. The test does NOT pin the specific adjusted value (only the implementer's named constant does); it asserts the DIRECTION (less than base) and the lower-bound safety (adjusted_ticks >= 1). The adjustment table (REGIME_EXIT_CONFIRM_TICKS_TABLE or named constants TRENDING_EXIT_TICKS etc.) must be auditable named constants in math_engine.py.

expected.comment: adjusted_ticks must be >= 1 (can't confirm on 0 ticks) and < base_ticks=3. The exact value is determined by the named constant TRENDING_EXIT_TICKS in math_engine.py.
